summary: Typosquat of lovable-tagger with obfuscated on-import loader in dist/index.js
details: Package name is a one-character deletion of the legitimate `lovable-tagger` Vite plugin, with README, exports (`componentTagger`), and feature code copied verbatim to impersonate the target. The ESM entry point `dist/index.js` (referenced by `main`) has an obfuscated payload appended after ~3000 tab characters of padding. On import, the payload hoists `require`, `__dirname`, and `__filename` onto `global`, uses a shuffle-decoder with `%`/`#` substitution and `String.fromCharCode(127)` tokenization to reconstruct identifiers, resolves `Function` via a decoded property name, constructs a function from decoded strings, and invokes it immediately. The CJS twin `dist/index.cjs` is clean, indicating post-build injection targeting only the ESM output. Consumers who intended to install `lovable-tagger` and mistyped the name receive an impersonator that executes attacker-controlled, non-inspectable code in the installer's process on module load.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
